headline: Antimicrobial Resistance Deaths Reach 4,500 Annually in Malaysia
context: AMR-related deaths increased 22% since 2022 as antibiotic misuse continues in both human and animal health sectors.

cards.big: 4,500 Malaysians died from antimicrobial-resistant infections in 2025
cards.sub: AMR deaths surpassed road accident fatalities for the first time last year

cards.big: 62% of antibiotics in Malaysia are sold without prescription at retail pharmacies
cards.sub: Over-the-counter antibiotic access accelerates resistance development

cards.big: Agricultural sector uses 78% of all antibiotics sold in Malaysia for animal growth promotion
cards.sub: Livestock antibiotic use creates resistant bacteria that transfer to humans through food

cards.big: Sweden banned non-therapeutic antibiotics in agriculture in 1986 cutting AMR rates by 45%
cards.sub: Early action on agricultural antibiotic use produced measurable public health gains

cards.big: Antibiotics losing effectiveness is a slow-motion crisis that will make routine surgery deadly
Should Malaysia ban non-therapeutic antibiotic use in livestock and enforce prescription-only sales
